mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in retail sales and housing starts reflect a broad-based demand shock that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a rank IC greater than 0.04 over any 24-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates robust supply-side capacity that outpaces inflationary pressures, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening periods yield negative 5-day forward returns in over 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index above 0.4% signals accelerating inflation that erodes real corporate margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above the 0.4% threshold consistently coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and consumer demand, directly boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or zero rank correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP coinciding with a monthly decline in retail sales confirms a recessionary demand shock, immediately pricing in lower earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over one week.
failure_condition: Concurrent GDPC1 and RSAFS declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals restored manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery periods yield negative 20-day returns in over 50% of cases.
